**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Fujian Putian Dexiang Machinery Industry Co., Ltd. presents a profile of an early 1990s foreign-invested (likely Taiwanese/Overseas Chinese) manufacturing venture that has functionally collapsed. Despite standard data corruption (where business scope was pushed to 'other_email' and workforce to 'organization_code'), forensic analysis clarifies its trajectory. Established in 1992 in Putian with a paid-in capital of 1.2 million USD, it originally aimed to manufacture 'critical auto/motorcycle parts, CNC machine tools, and mold standard parts'. However, the corrected workforce data reveals a mere 2 insured employees in 2024. This stark discrepancy between its highly ambitious, heavy-industrial scope (CNC machines) and its current 2-person micro-scale confirms that physical manufacturing has ceased entirely. It currently exists solely as a dormant holding shell, likely retaining its legacy licenses or land rights in Hanjiang District.
